def: The science of the derivation and signification of words; thatbranch of learning which treats of the signification and applicationof words.
An unnatural collection of serous fluid in any serous cavity ofthe body, or in the subcutaneous cellular tissue. Dunglison.
A Hebrew measure of capacity; a homer. [Written also core.]
To loose, or release, from, or as from, a cage.
A believer in the existence of phlogiston.
Damaged by accidental exposure to light; light-fogged; -- saidof plates or films.
Pertaining to, or derived from, nicotine; nicotic; -- usedspecifically to designate an acid related to pyridine, obtained bythe oxidation of nicotine, and called nicotinic acid.
Capable of being perceived; perceptible.-- Per*ceiv"a*bly, adv.
